transcript: Alright, you got a few people there?
Yeah, we got a few people here.
Alright, you all set for this, Steve?
Wait, let me ask you one quick question. Do we win some kind of prize?
Well, I guess you could. We've got circus tickets we can give you.
But, uh, you're gonna... you're gonna have to come down to Denver to see it.
[looped, pitch-shifted] We like to smoke, we like to smoke / Whitmans! Whitmans!

[singing] I like to smoke, baby / I do it every day / I like to smoke, baby / Don't care about tooth decay / I like to smoke, baby / I do it everyday / I like to smoke Whitmans / 'cause Whitmans are gonna kill you.
You got that gong out there?
That's gonna move right up the, uh, the Top Ten chart there, guys.
Well, uh... yeah, you're pretty darn proud of it, aren't you?
Alright, we've got a call now from, uh, Burbank, California. Hey, Tom!
Not bad. We wanted the circus tickets.
Uh huh, and we're prepared to earn 'em.
Let me get Steve Kroner on here for, uh... People for Smokers' Rights.
Okay, we've got, uh, we've got a couple of participants here.
Yeah. Alright, guys, uh... have you named this song?
Yeah, it's called "Smokin' Makes Me Feel Good".
Hey, wait, wait, what was the first song like?
I thought that one... I definitely thought that had some... had some high sound quality in it.
Yeah, it... it needed some work.
Alright, so anything will do better, I think, uh, Steve will probably agree, with what we, uh, had heard about, uh, twenty minutes ago.
Oh, you didn't like that one?
Alright. Uh, alright, this is, uh, Tom, and who else?
Alright, Tom and Flip in Burbank, California, and you know there's talent in California. Go ahead, guys.

[singing] Smokin' makes me feel good / Every time I light a cigarette / I time I smoke it / Every time I light a cigarette / Makes me feel so good / 'Cause we got high hopes, high hopes / Every time I see a Marlboro Red / Every time I smoke it / Every time I see a Marlboro Red / It does my heart some good / 'cause we like to smoke / We're smokers.
Well, I liked it. It's not bad. Uh, Steve, on a scale of one to ten, what do you suppose you'd give that?
Well, we're gonna have to... that one's a little more upscale, I think we might give that one an eight, and maybe even work it into the November program we have.
Yeah, I think so too. Uh, we have recorded that and we will probably play it, uh, back again, uh, periodically. Alright, uh, do you want these tickets, guys?
Alright, we're gonna give you four tickets to, uh, Ringling Brothers and Barnum and Bailey Circus, uh...
...at the Denver Coliseum, uh, it's October 9th at, uh, 7.30, and, uh, you guys originally from here, or what?
Yeah, well, yeah, well my parents live out there.
There's a lot going on, you know.
You've got, uh, 2 Live Crew, uh, in town for a special Halloween concert.
Yeah, alright, well... and, and you'll be able to, of course, uh, enjoy these wonderful circus tickets.
Okay, hang on the line, we'll get the information from you.
Alright. [sigh] My, my, my. Got some desperate listeners out there, Steve.
Oh, they're good at this time of the night.
